title: Semantic content review deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because Holistic page-purpose and semantic-content review requires human judgment across the complete artifact.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Semantic content review: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents misconception? Semantic elements automatically make the words inside them clear and accessible.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Review the actual language and task context; semantic markup supports but does not create understandable content.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Review the actual language and task context; semantic markup supports but does not create understandable content.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents correction rather than the documented misconception.
